# NJ Black Press Archive

> A historical database of Black publications in New Jersey from 1880 to the present. Maintained by the Center for Cooperative Media at Montclair State University.

## About

The NJ Black Press Archive catalogs 137 Black-owned publications spanning 145 years of New Jersey journalism history. It covers newspapers, magazines, newsletters, and digital outlets across 45+ cities from the post-Reconstruction era through today.

Data was curated by Amanda Alicea and led by Cassandra Etienne at the Center for Cooperative Media.

## Access

- Home: https://centercoopmedia.github.io/njblackpress/
- Full archive: https://centercoopmedia.github.io/njblackpress/archive.html
- GitHub repository: https://github.com/CenterCoopMedia/njblackpress

## Data

The complete dataset is available as JSON at:
- https://centercoopmedia.github.io/njblackpress/data/publications.json
- https://centercoopmedia.github.io/njblackpress/data/featured-publications.json

Each publication record includes: name, city, years of operation, format, frequency, mission statement, key staff, historical notes, archive URLs, and active/ceased status.

## Historical context

This archive documents:
- Black press history spanning post-Reconstruction (1880s) through the digital era
- Publications across Newark, Trenton, Camden, Jersey City, and 40+ other NJ cities
- Key periods: Reconstruction era, Great Migration, Jim Crow era, Civil Rights movement, Black Power era (1968 Newark uprising and aftermath), and contemporary digital journalism
- Publication types: weekly newspapers, monthly magazines, academic journals, community newsletters, digital-native outlets

## Related initiatives

- Black Publishers Collective (BPC): https://centerforcooperativemedia.org/programs/black-publishers-collective/
- South Jersey Information Equity Project (SJIEP): https://sjiep.org
- Center for Cooperative Media: https://centerforcooperativemedia.org

## Attribution

Data curator: Amanda Alicea
Project lead: Cassandra Etienne
Institution: Center for Cooperative Media, Montclair State University
